entities.name: SEED STORAGE
entities.description: The process of preserving seeds for future use

entities.name: VIABILITY
entities.description: The ability of a seed to germinate and grow

entities.name: REJUVENATION
entities.description: The process of restoring the vigor of stored seeds

entities.name: ORTHODOX SEEDS
entities.description: Seeds that can be dried to low moisture levels and stored at low temperatures without losing viability

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop used as an example of orthodox seeds

entities.name: FABA BEAN
entities.description: A legume crop used as an example of orthodox seeds

entities.name: LENTIL
entities.description: A legume crop used as an example of orthodox seeds

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: An environmental factor that must be controlled for successful seed storage

entities.name: MOISTURE REGIME
entities.description: An environmental factor that must be controlled for successful seed storage

relationships.description: Control of temperature is key to successful storage
Control of moisture regime is key to successful storage
Chickpea is a type of orthodox seed
Faba bean is a type of orthodox seed
Lentil is a type of orthodox seed
Orthodox seeds are the subject of the storage research
Storage conditions directly impact seed viability
Rejuvenation is a process related to the management of stored seeds
